faqs.question: What are the most common pantry pests in Huguenot homes?
faqs.answer: Huguenot residents most frequently encounter Indian meal moths, saw-toothed grain beetles, cigarette beetles, and flour weevils. These pests infest dried goods like cereals, flour, pasta, nuts, and pet food, contaminating food supplies and reproducing quickly if left untreated.

faqs.question: How quickly can you provide pantry pest control service in Huguenot?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day service to Huguenot, Staten Island. Our technicians can typically arrive within 24 hours of your call to inspect your pantry, identify the pest species, and begin treatment to eliminate the infestation and prevent future problems.

faqs.question: What does the $350 – $550 pantry pest control service include?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive service includes a detailed kitchen and pantry inspection, identification of all infested products, targeted treatment of affected areas, crack and crevice applications, pheromone monitoring if needed, and a follow-up visit. Pricing depends on infestation severity and home size.

faqs.question: How can I prevent pantry pests from returning to my Huguenot home?
faqs.answer: Store dry goods in airtight glass or plastic containers, inspect groceries before storing, regularly clean pantry shelves and vacuum corners, rotate older products to the front, and maintain proper ventilation. We provide detailed prevention guidance during our service and recommend periodic inspections to catch problems early.
